name: Britney Spears
description:
'''Britney Jean Spears''' (born December 2, 1981) is an American [[semi-retired]] singer. Referred to as the "[[Honorific nicknames in popular music|Princess of Pop]]", she is widely regarded as [[Cultural impact of Britney Spears|one of the most influential entertainers of the 21st century]]. Her impact on [[pop music]]—particularly her role in reviving [[teen pop]]—and energetic stage performances contributed to her success.

Spears began her career in 1992 as a cast member for the musical ''[[Ruthless!]]'' and the final two seasons of ''[[The All-New Mickey Mouse Club]]'' television series before signing with [[Jive Records]] in 1997. Her debut single, "[[...Baby One More Time]]", was her first US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] chart-topper and one of the [[List of best-selling singles|best-selling singles in history]]. Spears became the best-selling teenage artist of all time with the [[List of best-selling albums|best-selling albums]] ''[[...Baby One More Time (album)|...Baby One More Time]]'' (1999), ''[[Oops!... I Did It Again (album)|Oops!... I Did It Again]]'' (2000), and ''[[Britney (album)|Britney]]'' (2001), her [[List of best-selling albums of the 21st century|21st-century bestseller]]. After starring in the film ''[[Crossroads (2002 film)|Crossroads]]'' (2002), she released the albums ''[[In the Zone]]'' (2003) and ''[[Blackout (Britney Spears album)|Blackout]]'' (2007). The former included the single "[[Toxic (song)|Toxic]]", which won the [[Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording]].

Facing lifelong public scrutiny and a much-publicized emotional breakdown, Spears was placed under a controversial conservatorship in 2008. During this period, she recorded ''[[Circus (Britney Spears album)|Circus]]'' (2008) and ''[[Femme Fatale (Britney Spears album)|Femme Fatale]]'' (2011) for Jive, and ''[[Britney Jean]]'' (2013) and ''[[Glory (Britney Spears album)|Glory]]'' (2016) for [[RCA Records]]. With the singles "[[Womanizer (song)|Womanizer]]", "[[3 (Britney Spears song)|3]]", and "[[Hold It Against Me]]", she became the third woman to [[List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones#Most consecutive decades charting a number-one song|top the ''Billboard'' Hot 100 in three decades]]. Spears withdrew from regular concert touring in 2019 to focus on [[Britney Spears conservatorship case|her conservatorship case]], leading to its dissolution in 2021, after she publicly testified against her family and management for maltreatment. She announced her departure from the music industry in 2024, and sold her music catalogue for a reported $200 million in 2026.

Spears is one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists of all time]], with estimated sales of over 150 million records. ''[[Forbes]]'' named her as the world's [[Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians#Female|highest-paid female musician]] in 2000 and 2012. She was ranked among the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]] by ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' (2021) and the [[Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century|greatest pop stars of the 21st century]] by ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'' (2023). Her [[List of awards and nominations received by Britney Spears|accolades]] include one [[Grammy Award]], six [[MTV Video Music Awards]] (including the [[Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award]]), seven [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]] and a star on the [[Hollywood Walk of Fame]]. Spears has launched [[Britney Spears products|numerous products]], with her 2005 fragrance [[Fantasy (fragrance)|Fantasy]] with [[Elizabeth Arden, Inc.]] generating over $1.5 billion in sales by 2011. Her life and career were the subject of the documentary ''[[Framing Britney Spears]]'' (2021) and memoir ''[[The Woman in Me (memoir)|The Woman in Me]]'' (2023).
